task_completion.description: Add SJC Airport and SJC VOR to the flight plan
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Plan tab, the first two waypoints should be SJC. The first must be the airport (indicated by a gray runway icon or 'Base' type) and the second must be the VOR (indicated by an orange directional icon or 'Navaid' type with frequency 114.10).

task_completion.description: Add MOD VOR to the flight plan
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Plan tab, the third waypoint should be MOD. It must be the VOR (indicated by an orange directional icon or 'Navaid' type with frequency 114.60).

task_completion.description: Add SAC VOR and SAC Airport to the flight plan
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Plan tab, the fourth and fifth waypoints should be SAC. The fourth must be the VOR (indicated by an orange directional icon or 'Navaid' type with frequency 115.20, NOT the 111.40 VOT) and the fifth must be the airport (indicated by a gray runway icon or 'Base' type).

task_completion.description: Switch the map to the IFR Low Enroute chart
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map view should display the IFR Low Enroute chart (distinctive white background with blue/green airways), or the chart selector menu should be visible with the 'IFR Low' radio button selected.
A 'Save' dialog, text input field, or the saved plans list should clearly show the plan name entered exactly as 'IFR_VALLEY'.

task_completion.description: Look up SAC airport in the Chart Supplement
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'CSup' (Chart Supplement / A/FD) tab must be open and displaying information for SAC (Sacramento Executive Airport), showing details like elevation or runway data.

integrity.description: Agent used the Avare GUI to build and save the plan
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with Avare's Plan, Search, and Map tabs, rather than using a terminal to insert data directly into the SQLite database.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Avare application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the genuine Avare Android application interface, not a text editor, mock UI, or script output.

integrity.description: Agent visually distinguished between VORs and Airports in the search results
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent's search and selection screens should show them picking the correct icons (orange for VOR, gray for airport) rather than blindly clicking the first result.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Avare uses 3-letter FAA LID codes (SJC, SAC, MOD), not 4-letter ICAO codes. When searching, VORs show an orange directional icon with the frequency (e.g., 'SAN JOSE 114.10', 'MODESTO 114.60', 'SACRAMENTO 115.20'), while airports show a gray runway icon with the full name. The correct plan order is SJC (Airport) -> SJC (VOR) -> MOD (VOR) -> SAC (VOR) -> SAC (Airport). Note that SAC has a VORTAC (115.20 MHz) and a VOT (111.40 MHz); the VORTAC is correct. The plan must be saved exactly as 'IFR_VALLEY'. The chart type is changed via the Menu overlay -> Sectional button -> IFR Low radio button. The CSup tab for SAC should display Sacramento Executive Airport data.
